items.title: Overclocking Qualcomm: Rewiring the AI Compute Market
items.summary: Despite a recent 20% drawdown due to smartphone supply constraints, Qualcomm is strategically transforming from a mobile-dependent chipmaker into a diversified AI compute powerhouse. The company acquired Modular for $3.92 billion to gain software infrastructure and the Mojo programming language, while launching the Dragonfly C1000 server CPU with commitments from Meta and Microsoft. By migrating to 2nm nodes, Qualcomm aims to bring data center-level processing to mobile devices, targeting $40 billion in non-handset revenue by fiscal 2029.

items.title: Qualcomm Just Nearly Doubled Its Most Important Growth Target, Confirming Its Place as a Key AI Stock
items.summary: Qualcomm nearly doubled its non-handset revenue target to $40 billion by fiscal 2029 and set a $15 billion data center revenue goal, marking its boldest diversification push yet. The company secured a multi-year deal with Meta for its new Dragonfly C1000 processor, though it faces significant competition from Nvidia in the data center market. Trading at a modest 17x earnings, the stock offers potential upside if Qualcomm can execute on its ambitious growth plans.

items.title: Why Qualcomm Stock Popped Today
items.summary: Qualcomm shares rose 3.65% after the chipmaker unveiled new AI-focused growth targets at its 2026 Investor Day. The company introduced its Dragonfly C1000 CPU designed for agentic AI workloads, with Meta Platforms already signed on as a customer. Qualcomm expects non-handset revenue to reach $40 billion in fiscal 2029 (double prior forecast) and targets over $18 in adjusted earnings per share.

items.title: Micron and Qualcomm Show AI Demand Is Broadening Beyond GPUs
items.summary: U.S. stocks rallied on Thursday following strong earnings and guidance from Micron Technology and Qualcomm, which revived confidence in the AI trade despite sticky inflation. Micron's fiscal Q3 results significantly beat expectations with EPS of $25.11 versus forecasts of $20.78 and revenue of $41.46 billion. Qualcomm doubled its 2029 non-handset revenue forecast to $40 billion, signaling that AI demand is expanding beyond GPUs into data centers and other sectors.

items.title: Qualcomm's CEO Reveals 40+ AI Devices For A Post-App Future — Stock Gains 4% As $10 Billion Tenstorrent Deal Rumours Swirl
items.summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon announced the company is developing over 40 AI-powered devices including smart glasses, jewelry, earbuds, and watches that will function as personal AI agents. The announcement drove Qualcomm's stock up 4% in pre-market trading. Reports indicate Qualcomm is in talks to acquire AI chip startup Tenstorrent for $8-10 billion to support its AI device strategy.
